steps.title: Test equation I
steps.content:
Multiply the given equation by $1.5$:
$$1.5(4x+7y)=1.5(8)$$
$$6x+10.5y=12$$
Equation I is the same line, so it gives infinitely many solutions with the original equation.

steps.title: Test equation II
steps.content: For equation II, the coefficients are not proportional to the original equation's coefficients. The equations are different nonparallel lines, so they intersect in exactly one solution.

steps.title: Choose all valid equations
steps.content:
The system needs at least one solution. Equation I gives infinitely many solutions, and equation II gives exactly one solution.
The answer is C.
